steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The sentence explains the action is "difficult because weeds spread easily and quickly become unmanageable, making it hard to remove them completely." The blank must mean trying to stop the weeds' spread.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean stopping or blocking the spread of weeds. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — halting: "halting the spread of unwelcome weeds" — halting means stopping or bringing to a standstill. This directly fits the idea of preventing spread. ✓
  • Choice B — retrieving: "retrieving the spread" — retrieving means fetching or getting back. You cannot retrieve a spread; this is nonsensical in context. ✗
  • Choice C — saving: "saving the spread" — saving means preserving or rescuing. The passage wants to stop weeds, not preserve their spread. ✗
  • Choice D — pretending: "pretending the spread" — pretending means feigning or acting as if. This has no logical connection to controlling weeds. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
